title: elsholtzia fruticosa
intro: Elsholtzia fruticosa, also known as Vietnamese mint or wild basil, is a perennial plant native to Asia, characterized by its small white or purple flowers and strong, minty scent. The leaves and flowers of this plant have been used in traditional medicine for their antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems. Elsholtzia fruticosa is also believed to have various health benefits, including reducing stress, improving cognitive function, and protecting against certain diseases such as cancer and diabetes. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of Elsholtzia fruticosa can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often combined with other herbs to enhance their medicinal and therapeutic effects.
